title: Song of War

{{Quote|Launch all fighters! Knock out that Super Star Destroyer at the docks along with anything else you can get. I want this to be messy!|Isolder commands the ''Song of War'' over Dathomir|<ref name="Court" />}}
The '''''Song of War''''' was a [[Hapan Battle Dragon]] produced by [[Olanji/Charubah]] that served as both a [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] in the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium's]] [[Hapan Royal Navy|Royal Navy]] and [[Hapan]] [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Isolder]]'s [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] from [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] to at least [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]. Isolder used the red-[[Color/Legends|colored]] ''Song of War'' in 8 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]] to pursue the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] after Solo abducted [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. There, the ''Song of War'' [[Battle of Dathomir (Hunt for Zsinj)|encountered]] and engaged the forces of rogue [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]] while Isolder and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] followed Solo and his captive to the planet surface before the Battle Dragon departed the [[Dathomir system/Legends|system]]. Later, in 25 ABY, during the initial [[Standard year/Legends|year]] of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the ''Song of War'' led a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of Hapan [[Warship/Legends|warships]] to join the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] fight against the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. The Battle Dragon participated in a [[Battle of Fondor|battle]] at the planet [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]] that saw the majority of the Hapan war fleet destroyed by a blast from the [[Centerpoint Station]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]].

==Description==
A [[Hapan Battle Dragon]] produced by the company [[Olanji/Charubah]],<ref name="Chaos" /> the ''Song of War'' was a heavily-armed [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]]<ref name="Court" /> consisting of two saucers stacked upon one another and measuring 500 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length.<ref name="FF90" /> Like those on other Battle Dragons, the [[Cannon/Legends|guns]] of the ''Song of War'' could rotate around the edges of the saucers, allowing recharging guns to move out of optimal position in order to permit fresh guns to take their place; this gave the ''Song of War'' the ability to maintain a steady rate of fire,<ref name="Court" /> despite the use of archaic recharging Hapan weapons.<ref name="Chaos" /> With a [[Hull/Legends|hull]] [[Color/Legends|colored]] red, the ''Song of War'' had an armament consisting of torpedo tubes, [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], and [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]]. In [[Space warfare/Legends|battle]], the weapons of the ''Song of War'' could drain so much power that some corridors would only be illuminated by [[emergency light buoy]]s.<ref name="Court" />

The Battle Dragon also had a [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|deflector shield generator]], though [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] would be able to punch through the shields with their fire and potentially knock out the rotating gun emplacements. Equipped with a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] and a [[Hapan]] [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]], the ''Song of War'' could travel the sixty-four [[Parsec/Legends|parsecs]] from the [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] in seven [[Standard day/Legends|days]] with the help of a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] to navigate; without a Jedi, the ''Song of War'' could only make it in seventeen. The Battle Dragon had a [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]], which included a [[Viewscreen/Legends|viewscreen]] and was used as the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] control room, as well as an alarm system that was used to deliver warnings about potential threats. Possessing flight decks, the ''Song of War'' had a complement of starfighters—including [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Isolder]]'s personal fighter, [[Storm (Miy'til starfighter)|''Storm'']], and at least one [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]]—and had enough room in the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bays to accommodate the complement alongside an additional X-wing. The Battle Dragon was further equipped with [[Life support/Legends|life support]].<ref name="Court" />

==History==
===Battle with Zsinj===
{{Quote|Captain Astarta. Take over the attack. I'm going down to the planet.|Isolder gives command of the ''Song of War'' to Astarta|<ref name="Court" />}}
[[File:HapanBattleDragon-SotGSE.png|thumb|left|220px|The ''Song of War'' was an Olanji/Charubah Battle Dragon.]]
By the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]],<ref name="Court" /><ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and the remnants of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the ''Song of War'' served the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]]<ref name="Court" /> as a [[Hapan Royal Navy]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]]<ref name="Intrigue" /> under the command of [[Gender/Legends|Prince]] Isolder of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]]. In 8 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], the Hapans looked to unite themselves with the government of the New Republic by [[Marriage/Legends|wedding]] Isolder to [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], a prominent New Republic [[Politics/Legends|politician]]. However, former [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], who had feelings for Organa, abducted her and took her to the planet Dathomir<ref name="Court" /> in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Not willing to let Solo get away with his potential bride, Isolder decided to pursue the man and did so in the ''Song of War'', leaving behind the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] that Isolder's [[Parent/Legends|mother]], [[Ta'a Chume]], was taking to go after Solo. With the aid of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], who was willing to help Isolder because he believed that his sister, Organa, was in danger, the ''Song of War'' took only seven days to reach Dathomir, as the Jedi's [[Force power/Legends|abilities]] let Isolder take a more efficient route.<ref name="Court" />

Upon its arrival at Dathomir, the ''Song of War'' found an [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] that was being used by the forces of [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]],<ref name="Court" /> a former Imperial [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]],<ref name="Atlas" /> to repair his damaged [[Warship/Legends|warships]]; additionally, the docks were guarded by two Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]]. Using his Jedi abilities, Skywalker was able to locate Organa, and he informed the prince that she was safely en route to the surface of Dathomir. Isolder ordered the ''Song of War'' to launch all fighters to [[Battle of Dathomir (Hunt for Zsinj)|engage]] the enemy, attacking the helpless Imperial ships being repaired on the dock in order to get the attention of Zsinj's forces. Ceding control of the Battle Dragon to [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Astarta]] so that he could accompany Skywalker to the planet's surface to pursue Solo and Organa, Isolder ordered Astarta to continue fighting Zsinj's forces until the prince made it safely onto Dathomir. In addition, the prince commanded Astarta to warn Ta'a Chume's Hapan fleet of what to expect when they arrived at Dathomir after the ''Song of War'' retreated and to join the fleet in fighting against Zsinj.<ref name="Court" />

In response to the barrage of fire from the ''Song of War'', the two Star Destroyers backed away and spread themselves apart in order to force the Battle Dragon to fire on only one of the [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]]. However, Astarta chose a different path; she took the ''Song of War'' over to the orbital docks and pounded the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] [[Iron Fist (Executor-class)|''Iron Fist'']], which was being repaired there, while the two opposing capital ships retained their distance. While the ''Song of War'' distracted Zsinj's forces, fighting off starfighters launched from a pair of [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]] sitting in the shipyards, both Isolder and Skywalker safely made it onto the planet, and Asarta then withdrew the ''Song of War'' from the vicinity of Dathomir by taking it to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].<ref name="Court" />

===Yuuzhan Vong War===
[[File:IsolderEC.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Prince Isolder commanded the ''Song of War''.]]
The ''Song of War'' continued to serve as a flagship in the Hapan Royal Navy and was still under Isolder's command<ref name="Isolder" /> in the year [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]].<ref name="ERC" /> In that year, the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invaded]] [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and assaulted the New Republic. Losing ground to the invaders, the New Republic convinced the Hapans to enter the war on their side,<ref name="Chaos" /> and the ''Song of War'' server as the flagship of a Hapan force led by Isolder<ref name="Isolder" /> that was dispatched to engage the Yuuzhan Vong. The red Battle Dragon led a fleet of considerable size to the world of [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]], where the Hapan ships were to wait until the Yuuzhan Vong attacked the [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system]], as the New Republic had planned, where the Hapan fleet and other New Republic elements would then ambush the invaders.<ref name="Chaos" />

Instead, the Yuuzhan Vong duped them and attacked the [[Fondor Shipyards/Legends|shipyards]] of the planet [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]] in what came to be known as the [[Battle of Fondor]]. With the other New Republic fleets unable to reach the [[Fondor system/Legends|Fondor system]],<ref name="Chaos" /> the ''Song of War'' and the Hapan Royal Navy jumped to Fondor<ref name="Isolder" /> and prepared to engage the Yuuzhan Vong warships. However, both the Hapan Royal Navy and the Yuuzhan Vong fleet were devastated by a blast from the New Republic–controlled [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] [[Centerpoint Station]], which destroyed numerous vessels on both sides of the battle;<ref name="Chaos" /> the ''Song of War'', with Isolder aboard, was just outside the blast and managed to survive the destruction, though it had to limp back to the Hapes Cluster after the Yuuzhan Vong retreated from Fondor.<ref name="Isolder" /> Around two [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] after the disaster at Fondor, the ''Song of War'' was in a stationary orbit of the New Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], from where it was scheduled to take Isolder back to Hapes that day.<ref name="Chaos" />

==Commanders and crew==
The ''Song of War''{{'s}} commander was Hapan Prince Isolder,<ref name="Court" /> who managed to rise to a high level of distinction in the matriarchal society of the Hapes Consortium.<ref name="FF99">{{FactFile|99|ISO 1-2|Prince Isolder}}</ref> Despite being a prince, Isolder knew how to both command the Battle Dragon and [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] his own starfighter, ''Storm'', in combat against enemy forces.<ref name="Court" /> In 8 ABY,<ref name="ERC" /> Isolder's second-in-command was Captain Astarta, who was a loyal [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] for Isolder. Among the crew of the ''Song of War'' were those who served on the bridge of the red cruiser as well as the flight crews who prepared starfighters for launch on the flight decks.<ref name="Court" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Song of War'' first appeared in the [[1994]] novel ''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]'', written by [[Dave Wolverton]]. The Battle Dragon later made an appearance in the [[2000]] [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] installment [[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse|''Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse'']], by [[James Luceno]]. The ''Song of War'' has been mentioned in several sourcebooks, including both the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.
